method_atom: a deterministic routing process on a graph in which a mobile token is injected, then hops between adjacent nodes by following a local directional state at each node that the token itself updates by a fixed deterministic rule as it passes, so that on closed graphs the trajectory traces an Euler circuit and on open graphs the token eventually exits and a new one is injected. The commuting injection operators form an abelian group identical to that of an abelian avalanche/toppling process, and this equivalence yields the driven steady state and certain scaling exponents exactly.
object_atom: particles dropped at random on a lattice whose sites carry arrows, walking along those arrows and reorienting them, leaving through open boundaries — a lattice statistical-mechanics model of self-organised criticality, shown equivalent to the Abelian Sandpile model of toppling sand grains on the same graph.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the deterministic rotor-updating token process on a graph with an abelian group of addition operators granting exact solvability; the domain-specific object is the arrow-lattice/sandpile system of self-organised criticality in statistical physics.
